  1. Home
  2. Guides

Family Guides

Everything we know about choosing well — written for families under stress, not industry insiders. One topic per page, straight answers, no sales pitch.

Is it time for more help?

  • How to Talk to Your Parent About Senior Care Without a Fight

    How to talk to an aging parent about care or senior living: start early, listen more, offer real choices, align siblings first, and what to do if they say no.

  • Long-Distance Caregiving: How to Care for a Parent From Afar

    Long-distance caregiving for an aging parent: build a local check-in network, hire a care manager, use tech wisely, and plan a care runway before a crisis.

  • Overcoming the Fear of Senior Living: What to Expect Today

    Afraid of 'the nursing home'? That image is decades out of date. What modern senior living is really like, and how to verify a community's care quality.

  • Signs Your Aging Parent Needs Help: A Checklist for Families

    Worried about Mom or Dad? The warning signs an aging parent needs help — hygiene, falls, unpaid bills, isolation — and what to do next, step by step.

Making the decision

  • Finding Help Choosing a Senior Care Facility: Your Options

    You don't have to choose alone — how visiting, comparing, and free placement specialists work, who pays them, and how to get expert local help.

  • How to Choose a Senior Care Facility: A Step-by-Step Guide

    A step-by-step guide to choosing a senior care facility — assessing needs, budgeting, touring, checking records, and deciding as a family.

  • Pets, Couples, and Age Rules in Senior Living Communities

    Can the dog come? Can Mom and Dad stay together? Pet policies, couples pricing, and age requirements in senior living — the rules families forget to ask.

  • Senior Living Placement Agencies: How Advisors Get Paid

    Senior placement advisors are usually paid referral fees by facilities, not you. What that means for their advice, what to ask, and free options.

  • Senior Living Touring Checklist: What to Look For and Ask

    A practical senior living touring checklist — what to observe, questions to ask, red flags to catch, and why unannounced visits matter most.

Understanding care & facility types

  • Adult Family Homes and Small Residential Care Homes Guide

    Adult family homes offer care in a real house with 5-10 residents. Learn the benefits, trade-offs, and how to vet a small residential care home.

  • Assisted Living Explained: Care, Costs, and the Fine Print

    How assisted living really works: the care assessment, level-of-care charges above base rent, what staff can and can't do, and the discharge rules families miss.

  • CCRCs and Life Plan Communities: Contracts, Fees, Risks

    How CCRCs (Life Plan communities) work: Type A, B, and C contracts in plain language, entrance fees and refunds, and how to judge financial strength.

  • Hospice vs. Palliative Care: What Families Need to Know

    The difference between hospice and palliative care, what Medicare covers, what families actually receive, and why most wish they'd started sooner.

  • Independent Living for Seniors: What It Is and What It Costs

    What independent living includes, what it costs, and what it doesn't cover — a plain-spoken guide for families weighing this first step in senior living.

  • Levels of Care Explained: Independent Living to Hospice

    Every senior care level in one overview — independent living, assisted living, memory care, skilled nursing, respite, CCRCs, and hospice — with links to deep guides.

  • Medical vs. Non-Medical Services in Senior Living Explained

    What senior living communities actually provide — nursing, medication help, meals, rides, personal care — and what's included in rent versus billed extra.

  • Memory Care Explained: Costs, Quality, and When to Move

    What memory care provides for dementia and Alzheimer's, what it costs, when to move from assisted living, and how to spot truly good memory care.

  • Respite Care: Short Stays, Costs, and the Trial-Run Secret

    What respite care is, what short-term senior living stays cost per day, and how to use a respite stay as a trial run before committing to a community.

  • Skilled Nursing Explained: Rehab vs Long-Term Care Costs

    Skilled nursing homes explained: short-term Medicare rehab vs long-term nursing care, real monthly costs, Five-Star ratings, and choosing a facility fast.

  • Types of Senior Living Facilities: Sizes, Models, Costs

    Compare types of senior living facilities — large communities, small care homes, nonprofits, CCRCs — and how size changes daily life for your parent.

In-home care

  • Cost of In-Home Care: Hourly Rates and Monthly Budgets

    What in-home care really costs in 2025-2026 — hourly rates, honest monthly math at different hour levels, and what Medicare and Medicaid actually pay.

  • Hiring In-Home Care: Agency vs. Private Caregiver Guide

    How to hire in-home care for a parent — agency vs. registry vs. private hire, interviews, background checks, red flags, and being a fair employer.

  • Home Care vs. a Facility: An Honest Decision Framework

    Home care or a senior living facility? An honest framework — when aging at home works, the real tipping points, hybrid options, and how to decide.

  • In-Home Care Explained: Types, Schedules, and Services

    Understand the types of in-home care for seniors — companion care, home care aides, home health, and 24-hour care — and which fits your parent.

What it really costs

  • Cost of Senior Living: What Each Care Level Really Costs

    What senior living really costs in 2025-2026: assisted living, memory care, and nursing home prices, how care charges work, and how to compare quotes.

  • Hidden Fees in Senior Living: What Is Not in Your Quote

    The fees senior living quotes leave out: move-in fees, care level charges, supplies, and rate hikes — and how to get the full fee schedule in writing.

  • Senior Living Food and Dining: What Families Should Check

    How food and dining really work in senior living, why meal quality drives resident happiness, and how to evaluate the dining room on a tour.

  • Shared vs Private Rooms in Senior Care: Costs and Choices

    Shared vs private rooms in assisted living and nursing homes — real cost differences, when sharing works, Medicaid rules, and what you can negotiate.

Paying for care & government help

  • Government Programs That Help Pay for Senior Care Costs

    Medicaid waivers, PACE, VA benefits, and state programs can help pay for senior care. Learn what government help exists and how to actually get it.

  • How to Pay for Senior Care: The Complete Funding Toolkit

    Every way families pay for senior care: savings, home sale, long-term care insurance, VA benefits, and Medicaid — and why planning 2-3 years ahead matters.

  • Medicaid Estate Recovery: How States Reclaim Care Costs

    After a Medicaid recipient dies, states can claim repayment from the estate — including the family home. What to know before your parent applies.

  • Medicaid vs. Medicare for Senior Care: What Each Covers

    Medicare vs. Medicaid for long-term senior care: what each program actually covers, eligibility, spend-down, and the 5-year look-back, explained plainly.

Judging quality

  • Activities and Engagement in Senior Living: What to Look For

    How to judge activities and engagement in senior living — real calendars vs marketing, fitness programs, and what good memory care engagement looks like.

  • Reading Nursing Home Inspection Reports and Star Ratings

    How to read nursing home star ratings and inspection reports — what CMS Care Compare shows, what it hides, and where assisted living records live.

  • Staffing Ratios in Senior Care: What to Ask on Every Tour

    Why staffing ratios matter more than amenities in senior care — typical day and night numbers, turnover, agency staff, and how to verify answers.

The emotional side

  • Coping With the Decision to Move a Parent Into Senior Care

    Guilt, grief, and family conflict are normal when moving a parent into senior care. How to cope, find real support, and trust your caring choice.

Senior Living Community is an independent information resource for families. We are not a referral service and do not provide medical, legal, or financial advice — for decisions about care, consult the professionals involved. Facility details change; always confirm directly with a community. Last data review: 2026-07-11.
